content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Resolved both pending amendments from round-17 review for the success reconciliation handler (bead 9ni.8.5.1). Amendment 1: In claim_task (task_service.rs), added is_reconciliation_retry check based on the task's failure_class prefix 'reconciliation_'. Derived needs_remote_resume = is_failed_retry && !is_reconciliation_retry and passed needs_remote_resume (instead of is_failed_retry) to LeaseService::acquire. This causes try_resume_from_remote to be skipped for reconciliation-only retries (reconciliation_br_failed, reconciliation_metadata_error, reconciliation_milestone_update_failed), since the worktree branch already contains the completed work and the remote resume step would risk failing on unrelated network/auth errors before try_reconcile_success is reached. The worktree is still created (needed for lease management), only the remote resume step is bypassed. Amendment 2: Added workflow_run_id: Option<String> field to DaemonTask (model.rs) with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility. Added DaemonTaskService::set_workflow_run_id helper (task_service.rs). Added persist_workflow_run_id method to DaemonLoop (daemon_loop.rs) that reads the latest RunStarted journal event and persists the run_id on the task immediately after dispatch succeeds, before any post-completion work. In try_reconcile_success, modified the RunStarted event search to match the specific run_id from task.workflow_run_id when set, falling back to latest-event scanning for backwards compatibility with tasks dispatched before workflow_run_id was introduced. This prevents reconciliation retries from accidentally reconciling against a newer manual re-run's journal entries.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: In claim_task (task_service.rs:147-161), added is_reconciliation_retry check that tests whether failure_class starts with 'reconciliation_'. Derived needs_remote_resume = is_failed_retry && !is_reconciliation_retry. Changed the LeaseService::acquire call at line 178 to pass needs_remote_resume instead of is_failed_retry, so try_resume_from_remote in lease_service.rs:215-263 is skipped for reconciliation-specific failure classes
2. [Completed] Amendment 2a: Added workflow_run_id: Option<String> field to DaemonTask struct (model.rs:88-93) with doc comment explaining it is persisted after dispatch completes so try_reconcile_success can identify the correct run. Uses #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] for backward compatibility with existing serialized tasks
3. [Completed] Amendment 2b: Added workflow_run_id: None initialization to both DaemonTask struct literals in task_service.rs (create_task at line 85, create_task_from_github at line 705) and to the test helper in daemon_loop.rs (sample_waiting_task at line 3395)
4. [Completed] Amendment 2c: Added workflow_run_id: None to all 31 DaemonTask struct literals in conformance_spec/scenarios.rs, all 11 in tests/cli.rs, and 1 in tests/unit/automation_runtime_test.rs, preserving correct indentation at both 12-space and 16-space levels
5. [Completed] Amendment 2d: Added DaemonTaskService::set_workflow_run_id static method (task_service.rs:612-624) that reads the task, sets workflow_run_id, updates updated_at, and writes the task back to the store
6. [Completed] Amendment 2e: Added persist_workflow_run_id method to DaemonLoop (daemon_loop.rs:1599-1645) that extracts the run_id from the latest RunStarted journal event and persists it on the task via set_workflow_run_id. Skips if workflow_run_id already set. Best-effort: logs eprintln on failure but never blocks. Inserted call at both success paths: multi-repo at line 867 and single-repo at line 1518, both immediately after dispatch returns Ok(())
7. [Completed] Amendment 2f: Modified try_reconcile_success RunStarted event search (daemon_loop.rs:1716-1736) to use task.workflow_run_id when set: reads run_id_from_task, filters journal events to match the specific run_id instead of blindly taking the latest RunStarted. Falls back to latest-event scanning when workflow_run_id is None for backward compatibility
8. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix formatting in persist_workflow_run_id (rustfmt collapsed multi-line function signature and if-let block). Ran cargo test (757 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (0 new warnings in modified files, 16 pre-existing in unrelated files), cargo fmt --check (clean)

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 757 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (full suite)
- cargo test --lib success_reconciliation: 13 passed (8 unit + 1 e2e + 1 sync_failure_on_replay + 1 terminal_replay_tolerance + 2 hint tests), 0 failed
- cargo fmt --check: no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: 0 new warnings in modified files (task_service.rs, model.rs, daemon_loop.rs); 16 pre-existing warnings in unrelated files (fs.rs, cli/project.rs, diagnostics.rs)
- Amendment 1 verified: claim_task at task_service.rs:157-161 computes is_reconciliation_retry from failure_class prefix 'reconciliation_'; needs_remote_resume at line 161 is is_failed_retry && !is_reconciliation_retry; LeaseService::acquire at line 178 receives needs_remote_resume. Three reconciliation failure classes are covered: reconciliation_br_failed, reconciliation_metadata_error, reconciliation_milestone_update_failed
- Amendment 2 verified: DaemonTask.workflow_run_id field at model.rs:88-93 with serde(default); persist_workflow_run_id at daemon_loop.rs:1599-1645 extracts run_id from journal and persists on task; try_reconcile_success at daemon_loop.rs:1716-1736 uses task.workflow_run_id to filter RunStarted events; fallback to latest event when None
- Backward compatibility verified: serde(default) on workflow_run_id means existing serialized tasks deserialize with None; try_reconcile_success fallback (None => true match on latest RunStarted) preserves pre-existing behavior for tasks without workflow_run_id
- All 44 DaemonTask struct literals across 6 files updated with workflow_run_id: None — verified by grep count matching DaemonTask { construction count
- git diff --stat: model.rs +6, task_service.rs +30/-1, daemon_loop.rs +78/-11, scenarios.rs +31, tests/cli.rs +11, automation_runtime_test.rs +1 (total: 160 insertions, 11 deletions)

## Outstanding Risks

- The lineage task_id extraction still parses the 'task_id=<value>' format from outcome_detail via the extract_task_id_from_outcome_detail helper. This couples the fs adapter to a format defined in success_reconciliation.rs. A future refactor should thread task_id as a first-class parameter through the record_bead_completion/update_task_run chain to eliminate this format dependency.
- The next_step_hint.json file is never deleted by BvFailed — only overwritten by Captured or deleted by NoRecommendation. If bv is permanently unavailable for a milestone, a stale hint from a previous bead may persist indefinitely. This is acceptable since hints are informational and the selection step should treat them as advisory.
- The persist_workflow_run_id call is best-effort — if the task store write fails, reconciliation falls back to journal-based run_id extraction (the pre-existing behavior). For tasks where persist fails AND a manual re-run appends new journal events before retry, the fallback will pick the wrong run. This requires two simultaneous failures and is an extreme edge case.
- Amendment 1 bypasses try_resume_from_remote but still creates the worktree via create_worktree in LeaseService::acquire. For reconciliation-only retries, this worktree creation is technically unnecessary since no code will execute in it — but it is required for lease management (lease record references worktree_path). A future optimization could use a lightweight lease-only path that skips worktree creation entirely for reconciliation retries.
